title: Ipomoea celebica
taxonomy: Ipomoea celebica is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family Convolvulaceae. The genus Ipomoea consists of more than 600 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Ipomoea celebica belongs to the family Convolvulaceae, which also includes other popular species like Ipomoea purpurea and Ipomoea aquatica. This species is further classified into the tribe Ipomoeae, which comprises around 400 species of morning glories. The classification of Ipomoea celebica reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Ipomoea celebica, also known as the Celestial Morning Glory, is a perennial climber native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. This plant is rich in health benefits, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in the relief of muscle spasms, menstrual cramps, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea celebica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
